finro
HomeGuideComponentsExamples
Patterns

Examples

The three shells, what they're for, and what they look like.

standard shell

Sticky header + nav + container

The default. Use for: dashboards, reference pages, knowledge-base articles, blog-style content, landing pages. Site header stays fixed at the top; main content flows in a 1200px container below.

Best for
  • Multi-page KBs
  • Team wikis
  • Reference docs
  • Blog-style content
YAML pattern

shell: standard + components: [...] + optional nav_back

document shell

Centered card, print-ready

A single centered card (720px wide) with a teal-bordered header. Designed for content you'd print or PDF — meeting agendas, one-pagers, briefs, letters. Print CSS strips the dark theme and outputs cleanly.

Best for
  • Meeting agendas
  • One-pagers
  • Letters & briefs
  • Anything you print
YAML pattern

shell: document + eyebrow + subtitle → header bar. Body is components: [...].

deck shell

Full-viewport slides, keyboard-navigated

Full-screen presentation mode. Arrow keys (← → ↑ ↓) navigate between slides. Each slide holds its own components. The built-in "Download PDF" button uses print CSS to output each slide as a landscape page.

Best for
  • QBRs
  • Executive reviews
  • Pitch decks
  • Anywhere you'd use Keynote
YAML pattern

shell: deck + eyebrow + subtitle → top bar. Content is slides: [{label, components}].

Full site

Technical Success Hub

The original site that motivated finro. A customer portfolio dashboard linking to per-customer deployment guides, meeting agendas, and quarterly business reviews — all generated from ~300 lines of YAML.

Portfolio

standard

Dashboard of customer cards with health badges

Open →

Deployment Guide

standard

Interactive phase tracker with click-to-focus

Open →

Meeting Agenda

document

Centered card with print styling

Open →

Q2 Business Review

deck

Three-slide presentation with PDF export

Open →
